name: Urabe no Suetake
description: Urabe no Suetake, a valiant warrior and loyal retainer of Minamoto no Yorimitsu (also known as Raiko), is a prominent figure in Japanese folklore and warrior tales. He is renowned for his exceptional strength, courage, and unwavering loyalty. Suetake is often depicted as one of the 'Shitenno' (Four Heavenly Kings) of Yorimitsu, a group of elite warriors known for their supernatural abilities and bravery in battling demons and monsters. His origins are shrouded in some mystery, but he is believed to have come from the Urabe clan, known for their divinatory skills and warrior prowess. Suetake's most famous exploits involve confronting various Yokai, Oni, and other malevolent entities that plagued Kyoto during the Heian period. He wielded his katana with unmatched skill, often working alongside his fellow Shitenno, Watanabe no Tsuna, Sakata no Kintoki, and Usui Sadamitsu, to protect the capital and its inhabitants from supernatural threats. His dedication and bravery made him a symbol of loyalty and martial excellence, solidifying his place in Japanese legends as a true hero and paragon of Bushido. He embodies the ideals of a fearless warrior dedicated to protecting his lord and the innocent from evil. Suetake's stories continue to be retold and celebrated in Kabuki plays, Noh theater, and other forms of traditional Japanese art.
subtitle: Loyal warrior against demons and monsters.
achievements: Serving as one of the Shitenno

keyMyths.title: Defeating the Tsuchigumo
keyMyths.description: Urabe no Suetake is credited with helping to defeat the monstrous Tsuchigumo, a giant earth spider that terrorized the region. He used his skill and courage to overcome the creature's cunning and strength, ensuring the safety of the people.

keyMyths.title: Battles Against Oni
keyMyths.description: Suetake frequently fought against Oni, powerful demons that threatened Kyoto. His bravery and swordsmanship allowed him to vanquish these formidable foes, earning him the respect and gratitude of the populace.

keyMyths.title: Loyalty to Yorimitsu
keyMyths.description: Urabe no Suetake's unwavering loyalty to Minamoto no Yorimitsu is a central theme in his stories. He consistently put his lord's safety and well-being above his own, demonstrating the virtues of a true samurai.

sources.author: Helen Craig McCullough
sources.work: The Taiheiki: A Chronicle of Medieval Japan
sources.description: Provides historical context and mentions of warriors associated with Minamoto no Yorimitsu.
sources.text: Provides historical context and mentions of warriors associated with Minamoto no Yorimitsu.

sources.author: Richard Freeman
sources.work: The Kumaso Myth: A New Approach to Early Japanese Mythology
sources.description: Discusses the origins and significance of mythological creatures and heroes in Japanese folklore.
sources.text: Discusses the origins and significance of mythological creatures and heroes in Japanese folklore.
